title: Dynamic Workflows
description: Define JavaScript dynamic workflows and look up Factory Session API interface and configuration for you-agent-factory.
openingSummary: A dynamic workflow is a JavaScript (JAVASCRIPT) orchestrator-backed factory. The live run is a Factory Session — the same durable runtime object used for every live orchestration — not a separate DynamicWorkflowRun type.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: A dynamic workflow is a JavaScript (JAVASCRIPT) orchestrator-backed factory in you-agent-factory. Customers author JavaScript or TypeScript orchestrator source, then validate, start, and inspect the live run as a Factory Session. This documentation defines that idea in isolation, names the session nouns a live run depends on, and then documents the callable API interface and configuration knobs for source resolution and Model Context Protocol (MCP) serve modes.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A Factory is the authored orchestration definition a live session loads. A Factory Orchestrator (FactoryOrchestrator) is the orchestrator identity on that factory — Petri (PETRI) or JavaScript (JAVASCRIPT). Dynamic workflow is shorthand for the JavaScript kind. A Factory Session (FactorySession) is the canonical runtime instance for one live orchestration; a dynamic-workflow run is still that Factory Session with JavaScript orchestrator kind, not a separate DynamicWorkflowRun noun. A Dispatch is a shared execution request the session sends. A Factory Artifact (FactoryArtifact) is a session-owned durable output such as a result, finding, log, or checkpoint reference. A Factory Event (FactoryEvent) is a durable event-stream record for session lifecycle, dispatch lifecycle, progress, and artifacts.

apiInterface.title: API Interface
apiInterface.body: Dynamic workflows use the shared Factory Session API surface. Call the CLI workflow path to validate source and start or inspect a durable JavaScript Factory Session. Call Model Context Protocol (MCP) Factory Session tools from an MCP host. Read the same session through durable list, show, events, and preview style lookups. There is no separate dynamic-workflow resource type — every live run is still a Factory Session.

configuration.title: Configuration
configuration.body: Configuration for dynamic workflows covers workflow source resolution from a project root, source kinds such as WORKFLOW_NAME and INLINE_WORKFLOW, optional args and policy overrides, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) serve backing modes for fixture-backed versus runtime-backed hosts.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Start from the definition and key concepts to learn what a dynamic workflow is and which Factory Session nouns it uses. Open the API interface section when you need callable CLI, Model Context Protocol (MCP), or session inspection surfaces. Open the configuration section when you need source-resolution or MCP serve-mode knobs.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This documentation is a web reference for JavaScript dynamic-workflow API interface and configuration. It is not the Cursor first-loop guide, not the full Model Context Protocol (MCP) host catalog deep dive beyond dynamic-workflow needs, and not the Petri factory.json topology overview owned by the configuration documentation page.

cliSurface.body: Use the you workflow CLI path for JavaScript Factory Session work. Validate source before execution, start a durable session, then inspect status, result, dispatches, artifacts, and events for that same Factory Session id.

mcpSurface.body: Model Context Protocol (MCP) hosts discover Factory Session tools through you mcp serve. Prefer the canonical you.factory_session.* tool names. Compatibility you.workflow.* aliases resolve to the same handlers.

durableInspection.body: Durable inspection uses shared Factory Session reads. List or show sessions, read session-scoped events, and use the Factory preview contract for source validation without inventing a separate dynamic-workflow resource type.

links.cliValidatePurpose: Resolve and validate JavaScript workflow source without creating a Factory Session.
links.cliStartPurpose: Start one durable Factory Session asynchronously and return inspection links.
links.cliStatusPurpose: Read durable Factory Session lifecycle status and progress.
links.cliResultPurpose: Read the durable Factory Session final or partial result.
links.cliDispatchesPurpose: List durable Dispatch summaries for one Factory Session.
links.cliArtifactsPurpose: List durable Factory Artifact summaries for one Factory Session.
links.cliEventsPurpose: Poll ordered durable Factory Event history for one Factory Session.
links.mcpValidateSourcePurpose: Validate JavaScript orchestrator source through the Factory preview contract.
links.mcpStartSyncPurpose: Start one sync Factory Session and wait for terminal completion or timeout.
links.mcpStartAsyncPurpose: Start one async Factory Session for polling.
links.mcpGetPurpose: Read one durable Factory Session status and progress.
links.mcpGetResultPurpose: Read one durable Factory Session result.
links.mcpListDispatchesPurpose: List Dispatch summaries for one session.
links.mcpListArtifactsPurpose: List Factory Artifact summaries for one session.
links.mcpControlPurpose: Apply lifecycle controls such as pause, resume, cancel, or terminate.
links.mcpReadEventsPurpose: Read ordered Factory Session event facts.
links.inspectionSessionListPurpose: List live Factory Sessions with orchestrator kind on a running host.
links.inspectionSessionShowPurpose: Show one Factory Session projection, including JavaScript runtime fields when present.
links.inspectionEventsPurpose: Read the session-scoped Factory Event stream for one Factory Session.
links.inspectionPreviewPurpose: Validate source and policy through the Factory preview contract without creating a session.
